test_cases: POST /api/motivational-phrase with valid input ['hope', 'strength', 'courage'] returns motivational and satirical phrases
POST /api/motivational-phrase with less than 3 words returns 400 error
POST /api/motivational-phrase with more than 3 words returns 400 error
POST /api/motivational-phrase with empty string words returns 400 error
POST /api/motivational-phrase with non-string words returns 400 error
POST /api/motivational-phrase triggers satirical phrase timeout fallback
Rate limiting blocks requests after 30 requests per minute
Sanitization removes potentially malicious HTML from input and output
issues_found: The satiricalGenerator.js code snippet is included inside the main code string, which is not valid for runtime as a single file; it should be a separate module file.
The model name 'gpt-4o-mini' in satiricalGenerator might be a typo or invalid model name, which could cause API errors.
No explicit error handling for OpenAI API failures other than timeout fallback.
No tests or handling for cases where sanitize-html might remove all content, resulting in empty strings.
No unit tests or mocks for the external OpenAI API call, making automated testing difficult.
recommendations: Separate satiricalGenerator.js into its own module file and import it properly.
Verify and correct the OpenAI model name used in generateSatiricalPhrase.
Add more robust error handling for OpenAI API failures beyond timeout.
Implement unit tests with mocking for OpenAI API to enable reliable automated testing.
Add tests for sanitization edge cases to ensure no empty or unsafe outputs.
Consider increasing rate limiter configuration or providing a retry-after header for better client experience.
